title: pdftotext subprocess: file path passed unquoted, filenames with leading hyphen become pdftotext flags
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

**Dimension:** Security and Access / Functional Correctness (Input Handling)

**Location:** /home/doll/OpenClaudia/src/tools/file/read.rs lines 116-117 (pdfinfo), 153-155 (pdftotext)

**Violation:**
read_pdf_file invokes 'pdfinfo <path>' and 'pdftotext [-f F -l L] <path> -' with the path supplied as the next arg (line 116, line 153). Although Command::new().arg() correctly avoids shell metacharacter injection (no shell involved), it does NOT protect against pdftotext's OWN option parsing. A file named '-help' or '--version' or '-f' will be interpreted as a flag. More dangerously, a file named '-opw' (owner password) or '-upw' (user password) ACCEPTS an additional argument from argv, so a file named '--nocenter' could change behaviour, and a file named '-opw' would consume the literal '-' (stdout sink) argument at line 154, causing pdftotext to hang waiting for stdin. An attacker able to name a file (e.g. in /tmp, via git clone of an adversarial repo, via extracted archive) can abuse this.

Additionally:
1. **pdfinfo output parsing is loose.** Lines 121-134 do 'line.starts_with("Pages:")' — a PDF with 'Pages: 5 (hidden)' matches and count parses '5'; a PDF whose metadata title happens to start with 'Pages:' leads the parser.
2. **No bound on page count printed.** A PDF with 100 000 pages and page range '1-100000' generates unbounded output before the 100_000-char guard in read_text_file (not even applied here, line 164 returns raw pdftotext text).

**Mandated Refactor:**
1. Use '--' before the path argument in both pdfinfo and pdftotext invocations: cmd.arg("--").arg(path). pdftotext from poppler-utils >=0.75 respects '--' to terminate options; confirm version and document required minimum.
2. Canonicalize the path FIRST (fs::canonicalize) so it starts with '/', which pdftotext never interprets as a flag.
3. Parse pdfinfo output via exact regex: ^Pages:\s+(\d+)$ anchored at line boundaries.
4. Enforce a max output size on the pdftotext stdout (reqwest-style: write to a capped buffer).
5. Validate page-range arithmetic: reject last - first > MAX_PAGES_PER_READ (e.g. 200).
